headline: Transcript: Why Beijing still loves its five-year plans
summary: Jess Smith talks to Thomas Hale and Camilla Hodgson
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the FT News Briefing podcast episode: ‘Why Beijing still loves its five-year plans’

Jess SmithGood morning from the Financial Times. Today is Monday, October 20th, and this is your FT News Briefing.

President Trump’s latest meeting with Volodymyr Zelenskyy was a bitter pill for the Ukrainian president, and Chinese officials are cooking up their next five-year plan. Plus, investors are really digging rare earth stocks.

I’m Jess Smith, and here’s the news you need to start your day.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

US President Donald Trump told Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to accept the Kremlin’s terms or be, quote, destroyed by Russia. That’s the latest detail the FT is reporting about Friday’s White House meeting between the US and Ukrainian presidents. Zelenskyy had travelled to the White House to try and secure new long-range Tomahawk missiles, but the meeting descended into a shouting match. According to officials familiar with the matter, Trump told Z
